title: 'Water flowing one direction' — Aug 2018 analogy seed
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only Apple Note from Aug 23, 2018 (Yahir age ~15) — "Make water flowing one direction analogy for life….." The five trailing dots are the entire body. A self-tasking note: I should write this analogy out one day.
  • The to-do form is itself the artifact — Yahir at 15 was already cataloguing which metaphors he intended to develop into pieces of writing, treating his own future-essay output as a project pipeline. Same instinct that produces the 2024 07 04 questions mega-list six years later.
  • Water-flowing-one-direction is a low-entropy / unidirectional-time metaphor — sits adjacent to the cyclical time concept but argues the opposite: linearity, no doubling-back. The 2025 Emily déjà vu note revisits the same problem from the cyclical side. Two halves of the same teen question.
  • Companion to the same-year 2018 12 24 normal neutral to everything (Pokémon-type self-portrait) and 2018 12 01 yall if you are not obsessed with your goals choose new ones — the 2018 stub-cluster establishes Yahir's habit of naming a thought-shape before writing it out.
  • The analogy itself never gets developed in the surfaced corpus — making this an unresolved-promise note. Worth scanning later 2019-2020 notes for any "water" or "downstream" expansions.

Key quote

"Make water flowing one direction analogy for life….."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Earliest data point for the I should write this meta-task pattern — Yahir treating his own ideas as a backlog rather than thoughts that disappear when unspoken. This is the seed of the SecondBrain instinct seven years before this vault exists.
  • The unidirectional-water frame, if developed, would have argued no regrets / forward only — same energy as the "advance in this life and world" close of the September 2019 Phoenix flight letter. Likely it never got written because the energy crossed over into letter-form first.

Open questions

  • Did the analogy ever land in a paper, scholarship essay, or later note?
  • Pair against the cyclical time frame — which one wins in Yahir's worldview by 2025?

Wikilinks
